sourceMovie.title: Taken
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Taken (2008) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Armageddon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Taken clicked for you, Armageddon (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Predator 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Taken clicked for you, Predator 2 (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Taken?
faq.answer: Try Armageddon, Predator 2, Anna—each shares DNA with Taken while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Taken?
faq.answer: Taken (2008) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Taken part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Taken Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
